title: Round-the-clock operation launched to capture killer elephant in Nepal - Phnom Penh Post
description: Round-the-clock operation launched to capture killer elephant in Nepal  Phnom Penh Post
publisher.title: Phnom Penh Post
full_article:
CHITWAN – Chitwan National Park has deployed round-the-clock patrols in a renewed effort to capture Dhurbe, the notorious wild male elephant blamed for killing at least 25 people over the past 15 years, after it recently claimed the lives of a 25-year-old woman and her four-year-old son in Chitwan.

The joint operation, involving park officials, veterinarians, the Nepali Army and technicians from the National Trust for Nature Conservation (NTNC), has been searching for the elephant for the past week. However, officials say the animal has repeatedly evaded capture by remaining deep inside the forest during the day and venturing into nearby settlements at night.

The latest operation follows the July 4 attack in Jagatpur in ward 23 of Bharatpur Metropolitan City where Dhurbe killed 25-year-old Ashika Bote and her four-year-old son Bharat Bote after breaking into their home. The incident sparked protests by local residents, who demanded that authorities immediately control the tusker and provide relief to the bereaved family.

Since then, the elephant has continued to move across western Chitwan, damaging houses in Meghauli and, most recently, in Bankatta of Madi Municipality, where it destroyed the house of local Deepak Bote late on Friday night before retreating into the forest.

“We have deployed patrol teams in three shifts, operating round-the-clock, from the park headquarters,” said Dill Bahadur Pun, chief conservation officer at Chitwan National Park. “The teams are monitoring the areas from Jagatpur to Meghauli Golaghat and Madi, but the elephant has remained difficult to locate because it hides in the dense core forest during the day.”

According to Pun, poor visibility caused by dense vegetation and the monsoon weather has made tracking the elephant particularly challenging.

Authorities plan to tranquillise Dhurbe using a dart gun as soon as it is located. Officials will then fit it with a new satellite tracking collar and trim its tusks, which park officials believe could help reduce its aggressiveness.

“The new collar is far more advanced than the previous one,” Pun said. “It can transmit the elephant’s location as frequently as every 15 minutes and comes equipped with geofencing technology. If the elephant enters a designated high-risk area, the system immediately sends an alert, allowing our response teams to act quickly.”

The previous collar transmitted the elephant’s location only once an hour.

Officials said geofencing has already been programmed for villages stretching from Jagatpur to Meghauli Golaghat and from Bankatta to Bagai in Madi, where the elephant frequently strays into human settlements.

Dhurbe has been fitted with tracking devices three times before. A radio collar was first attached in November 2012 after authorities abandoned an attempt to shoot the elephant. Satellite collars were later fitted in 2020 and again in 2023, but the latest unit has deteriorated and no longer provides reliable signals.

Park information officer Abinash Thapa Magar said the upgraded collar would allow officials to monitor the elephant’s movements in real time through mobile phones and computers, enabling quicker deployment of response teams.

Dhurbe’s violent history stretches back to 2010, when it began attacking people around Chitwan National Park before expanding its range into Bara and Parsa districts. According to the park, the elephant has now killed 25 people, including Ashika and Bharat Bote.

Following the deaths of 15 people, the government ordered the elephant to be shot in December 2012 and spent around Rs1.6 million on a military operation to hunt it down. Despite being wounded, the elephant escaped into the dense forest and reappeared in western Chitwan in 2016.

As reported by the Post last week, Dhurbe has repeatedly devastated the family of Shanichara Bote. The elephant killed Shanichara’s parents in Madi in 2012, forcing the family to relocate to Jagatpur. Fourteen years later, it found the family again, and killed Shanichara’s daughter-in-law and grandson.

After Shanichara’s parents were killed, a high-level emergency security meeting chaired by the Chief District Officer issued a formal executive order to track down and kill the elephant. What followed was an intensive two-week operation involving the Nepali Army and conservation personnel in the dense forests of Chitwan.

Wildlife experts say fitting the elephant with a satellite collar remains the most practical short-term measure to protect nearby communities.

Hari Bhadra Acharya, senior ecologist and information officer at the Department of National Parks and Wildlife Conservation, said long-term solutions should also be explored.

“Continuous monitoring through satellite collars can help protect local communities, but a permanent solution could involve establishing a sanctuary for such aggressive elephants,” Acharya said.

He added that Nepal’s wildlife laws also allow authorities to destroy elephants officially declared rogue, although such a decision requires clear evidence and official approval.

Chitwan is also home to other wild bull elephants, including Ronaldo and Govinde, which conservationists say play an important role in breeding with captive female elephants. However, human-elephant conflict continues to claim lives in and around the park every year, as local communities press for a lasting solution.
translated:
चितवन – हाल ही म॑ चितवन म॑ एगो २५ साल के महिला आरू ओकरऽ चार साल के बेटा के जान लेबै के बाद पिछला १५ साल म॑ कम स॑ कम २५ लोगऽ के हत्या के जिम्मेदार ठहराबै वाला कुख्यात जंगली नर हाथी धुरबे क॑ पकड़ै के नयका प्रयास म॑ चितवन नेशनल पार्क न॑ चौबीसों घंटा गश्ती दल तैनात करी देल॑ छै ।

पार्क के अधिकारी, पशु चिकित्सक, नेपाली सेना आ नेशनल ट्रस्ट फॉर नेचर कंजर्वेशन (एनटीएनसी) के तकनीशियन के शामिल संयुक्त अभियान में पिछला एक सप्ताह स हाथी के खोज भ रहल अछि। हालांकि अधिकारी के कहनाय छनि जे ई जानवर दिन मे जंगल के गहराई मे रहि कs आ राति मे पास के बस्ती मे उतरि कए बेर-बेर पकड़य सं बचैत रहल अछि.

ताजा अभियान भरतपुर महानगर के वार्ड 23 के जगतपुर मे 4 जुलाई के हमला के बाद भेल अछि जतय धुरबे 25 वर्षीय आशिका बोटे आओर हुनकर चारि साल के बेटा भरत बोटे के घर मे घुसला के बाद हत्या क देलक। एहि घटना के बाद स्थानीय निवासी के विरोध भेल, जे अधिकारी तुरंत टस्कर पर नियंत्रण राखय आ शोक संतप्त परिवार के राहत देबय के मांग केलखिन्ह.

तहिया सँ हाथी पश्चिमी चितवन के पार घुमैत रहल अछि, जाहि सं मेघौली आ हाल मे माडी नगरपालिका के बांकाट्टा मे घर के नुकसान पहुंचल अछि, जतय शुक्रदिन देर राति स्थानीय दीपक बोटे के घर के नष्ट क देलक आ फेर जंगल मे पाछू हटि गेल.

चितवन नेशनल पार्क के मुख्य संरक्षण अधिकारी डिल बहादुर पुन कहलनि जे, हम पार्क मुख्यालय सं तीन पाली मे गश्ती दल के तैनात केने छी, जे चौबीसो घंटा संचालित अछि। “टीम जगतपुर स मेघौली गोलाघाट आ माडी तक के इलाका पर नजरि राखि रहल अछि, मुदा हाथी के पता लगाबय मे दिक्कत बनल अछि किएक त ओ दिन मे घना कोर जंगल मे नुकायल रहैत अछि।”

पुन के अनुसार घना वनस्पति आरू मानसून के मौसम के कारण खराब दृश्यता के कारण हाथी के पता लगाना विशेष रूप स॑ चुनौतीपूर्ण होय गेलऽ छै ।

अधिकारि सब धुरबे के पता चलैत देरी डार्ट गन के इस्तेमाल सं शांत करय के योजना बना रहल छथिन्ह. एकरऽ बाद अधिकारी एकरा म॑ नया सैटेलाइट ट्रैकिंग कॉलर फिट करी क॑ एकरऽ दांत क॑ ट्रिम करतै, जेकरा स॑ पार्क केरऽ अधिकारियऽ के मानना ​​छै कि एकरऽ आक्रामकता क॑ कम करै म॑ मदद मिल॑ सकै छै ।

पुन कहलनि जे, नवका कॉलर पहिने सं कहीं बेसी उन्नत अछि। “ई हर १५ मिनट म॑ हाथी केरऽ स्थान क॑ बार-बार संचारित करी सकै छै आरू जियोफेंसिंग तकनीक स॑ लैस आबै छै । अगर हाथी कोनो निर्धारित उच्च जोखिम वाला क्षेत्र म॑ प्रवेश करै छै त॑ सिस्टम तुरंत अलर्ट भेजै छै, जेकरा स॑ हमरऽ प्रतिक्रिया टीम जल्दी स॑ कार्रवाई करी सकै छै ।”

पहिने के कॉलर हाथी के स्थान के संचार घंटा में मात्र एक बेर करैत छल.

अधिकारी सब के कहनाय छनि जे जगतपुर सं ल क मेघौली गोलाघाट आओर माडी के बांकट्टा सं बगाई तक के गाम के लेल जियोफेंसिंग के कार्यक्रम पहिने सं कएल गेल अछि, जतय हाथी अक्सरहां मानव बस्ती मे भटकैत अछि.

धुरबे मे एहि स पहिने तीन बेर ट्रैकिंग डिवाइस लगाउल गेल अछि। हाथी क॑ गोली मारै के कोशिश क॑ अधिकारियऽ न॑ छोड़ला के बाद नवंबर २०१२ म॑ पहलऽ बार रेडियो कॉलर लगाय देलऽ गेलऽ छेलै । बाद म॑ २०२० म॑ आरू फेरू २०२३ म॑ सैटेलाइट कॉलर लगाय देलऽ गेलै, लेकिन नवीनतम यूनिट खराब होय गेलऽ छै आरू अब॑ विश्वसनीय संकेत नै दै छै ।

पार्क के सूचना अधिकारी अबिनाश थापा मगर के कहनाय छनि जे अपग्रेड कॉलर सं अधिकारी मोबाइल फोन आओर कंप्यूटर के माध्यम सं हाथी के हरकत पर रियल टाइम निगरानी क सकय छथिन्ह, जाहि सं रिस्पांस टीम के जल्दी सं तैनाती भ सकय.

धुर्बे केरऽ हिंसक इतिहास २०१० स॑ शुरू होय छै, जब॑ ई न॑ बारा आरू पर्सा जिला म॑ अपनऽ क्षेत्र बढ़ाबै स॑ पहल॑ चितवन नेशनल पार्क के आसपास के लोगऽ प॑ हमला करना शुरू करलकै । पार्क के अनुसार हाथी आब आशिका आ भरत बोटे सहित 25 लोक के मारि देलक अछि।

15 लोक के मौत के बाद सरकार दिसंबर 2012 मे हाथी के गोली मारय के आदेश देलक आओर ओकर शिकार के लेल सैन्य अभियान मे करीब 16 लाख रुपया खर्च केलक. घायल भेलाक बादो हाथी सघन जंगल मे भागि गेल आ 2016 मे पश्चिमी चितवन मे फेर स देखबा मे आयल।

जेना कि पिछला सप्ताह पोस्ट के खबर अछि जे धुरबे बेर-बेर शनिचर बोटे के परिवार के तबाह क देलक अछि. हाथी 2012 मे माडी मे शनिचर के माता-पिता के हत्या क देने छल, जाहि सं परिवार के जगतपुर स्थानांतरित करय पड़ल छल. चौदह बर्खक बाद फेर परिवार भेटि गेलै, आ शनिचरक पुतहु आ पोता केँ मारि देलकैक |

शनिचर केरऽ माता-पिता केरऽ हत्या के बाद मुख्य जिला अधिकारी के अध्यक्षता म॑ उच्च स्तरीय आपातकालीन सुरक्षा बैठक म॑ हाथी के पता लगाबै आरू ओकरा मारै के औपचारिक कार्यकारी आदेश जारी करलऽ गेलै । एकर बाद जे भेल से चितवन के सघन जंगल मे नेपाली सेना आ संरक्षणकर्मी के शामिल करय वाला दू सप्ताह के गहन अभियान छल.

वन्यजीव विशेषज्ञऽ के कहना छै कि हाथी क॑ उपग्रह कॉलर फिट करना नजदीकी समुदायऽ के सुरक्षा लेली सबसें व्यावहारिक अल्पकालिक उपाय बनलऽ छै ।

राष्ट्रीय उद्यान आरू वन्यजीव संरक्षण विभाग केरऽ वरिष्ठ पारिस्थितिकीविद आरू सूचना अधिकारी हरि भद्र आचार्य न॑ कहलकै कि एकरऽ दीर्घकालिक समाधान भी खोजना चाहियऽ ।

आचार्य नँ कहलकै, “उपग्रह कॉलर के माध्यम स॑ लगातार निगरानी करला स॑ स्थानीय समुदायऽ के सुरक्षा म॑ मदद मिल॑ सकै छै, लेकिन एकरऽ स्थायी समाधान म॑ ऐसनऽ आक्रामक हाथी लेली अभयारण्य के स्थापना शामिल होय सकै छै ।

हुनी आरू कहलकै कि नेपाल केरऽ वन्यजीव कानून स॑ अधिकारियऽ क॑ आधिकारिक तौर प॑ बदमाश घोषित हाथी क॑ नष्ट करै के अनुमति भी मिलै छै, हालांकि ऐसनऽ फैसला लेली स्पष्ट सबूत आरू आधिकारिक मंजूरी के जरूरत छै ।

चितवन म॑ रोनाल्डो आरू गोविंडे सहित अन्य जंगली बैल हाथी भी रहै छै, जेकरऽ संरक्षणकर्ता सिनी के कहना छै कि कैद मादा हाथी के साथ प्रजनन म॑ अहम भूमिका निभाबै छै । लेकिन, मानव-हाथी के संघर्ष हर साल पार्क आरू आसपास के लोगऽ के जान लेत॑ रहै छै, कैन्हेंकि स्थानीय समुदाय एकरऽ स्थायी समाधान लेली दबाव बनाबै छै ।

title: Nepal's conservation debate shifts from wildlife to people - Everest Chronicle
description: Nepal's conservation debate shifts from wildlife to people  Everest Chronicle
publisher.title: Everest Chronicle
full_article:
For decades Nepal has been celebrated as a global conservation success story. National parks have helped populations of tigers, rhinos and other endangered wildlife recover, while protected areas now cover nearly a quarter of the country. Yet conservationists increasingly argue that the next phase of protecting nature will depend less on expanding parks than on recognising the people who have long lived alongside them.

The argument emerged from a roundtable discussion in Lalitpur on Friday, where around 40 researchers, conservationists, academics and civil society representatives gathered to debate the future of Nepal's biodiversity. The event, titled "Nepal's Conservation at a Crossroads: What Next?", was organised by the Society for Conservation Biology (SCB) Nepal with support from the National Trust for Nature Conservation (NTNC).

Participants argued that biodiversity conservation cannot succeed without integrating indigenous peoples' traditional knowledge, livelihoods and cultural practices into environmental policies. They said existing laws and programmes often overlook local communities, while poor coordination among Nepal's three tiers of government has weakened implementation of conservation policies.

The discussion reflected a broader debate playing out across the world: whether conservation should prioritise strict protection of landscapes or embrace approaches that recognise communities as stewards of nature rather than threats to it.

Prakash Kumar Paudel, president of SCB Nepal, said conservation challenges in Nepal are inherently multidisciplinary and require cooperation across scientific, social and policy sectors. He argued that policymakers have paid insufficient attention to the relationship between forests, local communities and human-wildlife conflict, leaving important social dimensions out of conservation planning.

Uttam Babu Shrestha, founding director of the Global Institute for Interdisciplinary Studies Nepal, criticised what he described as the criminalisation of indigenous communities' traditional practices while large infrastructure projects continue to receive government approval. Conservation strategies, he argued, should distinguish between activities that genuinely threaten ecosystems and long-standing cultural practices that have coexisted with nature for generations.

Former Lumbini Province forestry secretary Pashupatinath Koirala called for stronger evidence-based policymaking, saying Nepal needs more research to evaluate whether existing models—including buffer zones and protected areas—are achieving their intended ecological and social outcomes.

The consensus emerging from the discussion was that Nepal's conservation achievements are real but may prove difficult to sustain unless future policies move beyond protecting landscapes to empowering the communities that inhabit them. As climate change, infrastructure development and competing land-use pressures intensify, participants argued that conservation will depend as much on governance and inclusion as on ecological science.
translated:
दशकों स नेपाल वैश्विक संरक्षण सफलताक कथाक रूप मे मनाओल जाइत अछि । राष्ट्रीय उद्यान न॑ बाघ, गैंडा आरू अन्य विलुप्त होय रहलऽ वन्यजीवऽ के आबादी क॑ ठीक होय म॑ मदद करलकै, जबकि संरक्षित क्षेत्र अब॑ देश केरऽ लगभग एक चौथाई हिस्सा क॑ कवर करै छै । तइयो संरक्षणवादी सब के तर्क बढ़ैत अछि जे प्रकृति के रक्षा के अगिला चरण पार्क के विस्तार पर कम निर्भर करत, नै कि ओहि लोक के पहचान करय पर जे बहुत दिन स ओकर बगल में रहैत अछि.

ई तर्क शुक्रदिन ललितपुर मे गोलमेज चर्चा स सामने आयल अछि, जतय करीब 40 शोधकर्ता, संरक्षणवादी, शिक्षाविद आ नागरिक समाज के प्रतिनिधि नेपाल के जैव विविधता के भविष्य पर बहस करय लेल जमा भेल छल। "एक चौराहा पर नेपाल के संरक्षण: आगू की?"

प्रतिभागी सब के तर्क छेलै कि जैव विविधता संरक्षण बिना आदिवासी लोगऽ के पारंपरिक ज्ञान, आजीविका आरू सांस्कृतिक प्रथा क॑ पर्यावरण नीति म॑ शामिल करलऽ सफल नै होय सकै छै । ओ सब कहलनि जे, मौजूदा कानून आ कार्यक्रम मे प्रायः स्थानीय समुदाय के अनदेखी कयल जाइत अछि, जखन कि नेपाल के तीन स्तर के सरकार के बीच खराब समन्वय के कारण संरक्षण नीति के क्रियान्वयन कमजोर भ गेल अछि।

ई चर्चा म॑ पूरा दुनिया म॑ खेललऽ जाय रहलऽ एगो व्यापक बहस के झलक मिललै कि की संरक्षण क॑ परिदृश्य केरऽ सख्त सुरक्षा क॑ प्राथमिकता देना चाहियऽ या ऐन्हऽ दृष्टिकोण क॑ अपनाना चाहियऽ जे समुदाय क॑ प्रकृति केरऽ भंडारी के रूप म॑ पहचानै छै, नै कि ओकरा प॑ खतरा के रूप म॑ ।

एससीबी नेपालक अध्यक्ष प्रकाश कुमार पौडेल नेपालमे संरक्षणक चुनौती स्वभावतः बहुविषयक अछि आ वैज्ञानिक, सामाजिक आ नीति क्षेत्रमे सहयोगक आवश्यकता रहल बतौलनि । हुनकऽ तर्क छेलै कि नीति निर्माता न॑ जंगल, स्थानीय समुदाय आरू मानव-वन्यजीव संघर्ष के बीच संबंध प॑ पर्याप्त ध्यान नै देल॑ छै, जेकरा स॑ महत्वपूर्ण सामाजिक आयाम संरक्षण योजना स॑ बाहर होय गेलऽ छै ।

ग्लोबल इंस्टीट्यूट फॉर इंटरडिसिप्लिनरी स्टडीज नेपाल के संस्थापक निदेशक उत्तम बाबू श्रेष्ठ आदिवासी समुदाय के पारम्परिक प्रथा के अपराधीकरण के बात के आलोचना केलनि जखन कि पैघ बुनियादी ढांचा परियोजना के सरकारी मंजूरी भेटैत रहैत अछि। हुनकऽ तर्क छेलै कि संरक्षण रणनीति म॑ ऐन्हऽ गतिविधियऽ म॑ अंतर करलऽ जाना चाहियऽ जे वास्तव म॑ पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र क॑ खतरा म॑ डालै छै आरू लंबा समय स॑ चलै वाला सांस्कृतिक प्रथा जे पीढ़ी-दर-पीढ़ी स॑ प्रकृति के साथ सह-अस्तित्व म॑ छै ।

लुम्बिनी प्रांत केरऽ पूर्व वानिकी सचिव पशुपतिनाथ कोइराला न॑ कहलकै कि नेपाल क॑ ई मूल्यांकन करै लेली आरू शोध के जरूरत छै कि बफर जोन आरू संरक्षित क्षेत्र सहित मौजूदा मॉडल अपनऽ इच्छित पारिस्थितिकी आरू सामाजिक परिणाम प्राप्त करी रहलऽ छै कि नै ।

title: Karnataka man killed in Nepal, roommate accused of knife attack - Firstpost
description: Karnataka man killed in Nepal, roommate accused of knife attack  Firstpost
publisher.title: Firstpost
full_article:
A 25-year-old man from Karnataka’s Belagavi, Sahil Riyaz Ahmed Momin, has died from stab wounds in Nepal’s Jhapa district.

Indian and Nepali authorities suspect that Sahil’s roommate, Saddam Hussain, stabbed him following an argument. Saddam is currently at large.

A native of Uttar Pradesh, Saddam is suspected to have fled from Nepal into UP, according to The New Indian Express.

Belagavi police have shared Saddam’s photograph and other relevant details with the UP Police to assist in apprehending him.

STORY CONTINUES BELOW THIS AD

Victim stabbed after argument

Sahil worked at a hotel in Jhapa. After settling there, he invited a friend from Belagavi to join him for work.

The friend stayed with Sahil and his roommate Saddam.

That evening, Sahil and Saddam got into an argument, but the situation was temporarily resolved with the intervention of the friend.

More from World Indian student killed in Uzbekistan; family alleges torture, forced conversion attempt What are Bharat Gaurav tourist trains? Routes, fares and booking details explained

Later that night, however, Saddam allegedly attacked Sahil with a knife while he was asleep. Sahil’s friend reportedly tried to catch Saddam but he managed to flee. The Nepali authorities have launched a search for him.

The New Indian Express reported that Sahil was rushed to hospital but succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment.

Sahil’s family has been in touch with Belagavi police and is coordinating with Nepali authorities to complete the necessary formalities and bring his body back to India for the last rites. The New Indian Express reported that Sahil’s father, brother, and other relatives have left for Nepal to bring the body home.

Family and friends told the newspaper that Sahil was a calm and soft-spoken young man. He had previously worked in Dubai and Thailand before moving to Nepal around 25 days before his death.

title: Beyond handshakes: Nepal needs agenda-led diplomacy - The Himalayan Times
description: Beyond handshakes: Nepal needs agenda-led diplomacy  The Himalayan Times
publisher.title: The Himalayan Times
full_article:
Courtesy may open diplomatic doors, and protocol may preserve the dignity of the state, but neither is enough to protect Nepal's national interest. Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal's visits to India and China should therefore not be judged only by who received him, how warmly he was welcomed, or what statements were issued. They should be judged by what Nepal prepared before departure, what it placed on the table, and what it followed up after return.

The visits came as the new Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP)-led government was trying to define its foreign-policy style, reassure both neighbours, and move beyond early diplomatic controversies. The question is not whether the visits were necessary. They were. The more important question is whether Nepal went with structured agendas and returned with measurable follow-up.

Related Articles Stop splurging, start snowballing: The journey from the Marshmallow Effect to the Hockey Stick Effect Final day of group play at the World Cup sets the field for the round of 32

For Nepal, engagement with both neighbours is unavoidable. But Nepal does not lack diplomatic visits. What it lacks is a visible system of responsibilities, timelines and measurable action after those visits.

This matters especially for a government that came to power promising a break from old politics. Prime Minister Balendra Shah has appeared determined to introduce a more controlled diplomatic style, with greater emphasis on protocol, institutional channels, and state-led engagement with foreign diplomats. Indeed, Nepal needs a professional diplomatic culture. State-to-state relations must be conducted through institutions, records, protocol, and national priorities.

googletag.cmd.push(function() { googletag.display('div-gpt-ad-1686127029429-0'); }); googletag.cmd.push(function() { googletag.display('div-gpt-ad-1686127067910-0'); }); googletag.cmd.push(function() { googletag.display('div-gpt-ad-1686127112906-0'); });

But protocol alone is not diplomacy. Protocol may protect dignity, but only a structured agenda protects national interest. The timing of the visits made this clearer. Before them, the government was already under diplomatic scrutiny. The Prime Minister's reported remarks on Nepal-India border issues created controversy, including debate over encroachment and the possible role of British-era historical records. Later clarifications said Nepal was not seeking third-party mediation. By then, however, the remarks had triggered debate inside Nepal and a response from India rejecting third-party involvement.

Against this background, RSP president Rabi Lamichhane's visit to India, followed by the Foreign Minister's official visits to India and China, deserves closer assessment.

The India visit provided an opportunity to reassure India that Nepal's new political style would remain within responsible statecraft, while shifting the conversation towards practical areas such as trade, investment, connectivity, energy, and people-to-people relations. The China visit also mattered.

After visible engagement with India, outreach to Beijing was necessary to communicate continuity, balance, and respect for Nepal's northern neighbour. It covered broad areas including infrastructure, connectivity, trade, investment, tourism, agriculture, energy, technology, digitalisation, and implementation of past agreements.

Both visits had value. They kept channels open, reduced uncertainty, and introduced the new government's external posture. But the central question remains: were they guided by sufficiently structured and mutually understood agendas, or were they useful but broadly framed diplomatic engagements with limited public evidence of follow-up?

The public record shows broad agendas, selected outputs, and general commitments. For India, the stated areas included trade, investment, connectivity, energy, and people-to-people ties. Later remarks referred to digital cooperation, cross-border payment systems, educational partnerships, emerging technologies, artificial intelligence, and regional language technologies. For China, the public record referred to cooperation across infrastructure, connectivity, border management, energy, trade, investment, agriculture, technology transfer, digitalisation, tourism and people-to-people relations, along with commitment to implement past programmes and projects.

These are important. But broad themes, selected outputs and general commitments are not the same as a publicly disclosed follow-up plan. The available public record does not yet show a structured action matrix for either visit identifying responsible agencies, counterpart institutions, timelines, and measurable next steps. That gap is precisely why Nepal needs agenda-led diplomacy.

A serious high-level diplomatic visit should answer five questions before departure. What exactly does Nepal want from each visit? Has the agenda been discussed and broadly agreed with the other side? Which issues are ready for decision, technical review, or political signalling? Who will follow up after return? How will the Parliament and citizens know whether the visit produced results?

For India, broad discussion must become concrete workstreams: power trade, market access, customs facilitation, digital payments, border infrastructure, air routes, and the practical needs of Nepalis in India. Border issues should be handled through evidence, institutions, and disciplined language.

For China, the same discipline is needed. If trade deficit, tourism, infrastructure or past agreements are raised, Nepal must identify priority actions, assess viability, understand delays and assign responsibility.

The Foreign Minister's visits should therefore begin a new standard in which every major foreign visit produces a post-visit action matrix. Such a matrix need not disclose sensitive details. But it should list issues discussed, responsible Nepali agencies, counterpart institutions, next steps, timelines, and matters requiring further negotiation. This is how diplomacy becomes governance.

Older parties also conducted foreign visits, issued statements, and signed agreements that often remained unimplemented. The RSP-led government should not repeat that culture with new faces.

Nepal needs to engage India with confidence, China with clarity, and both neighbours from the standpoint of national interest. What weakens Nepal is not engagement with either neighbour, but engagement without preparation, statements without discipline, and visits without follow-up.

The future test of Nepal's diplomacy should not be whether leaders are received warmly in New Delhi or Beijing. The test should be whether visits were based on agreed agendas, commitments were followed up, and citizens saw results after the delegation returned.

Ghale is a former member of the WHO Governance Team in Geneva with an interest in governance, policy, and diplomacy

शिष्टाचार कूटनीतिक दरवाजा खोलि सकैत अछि, आ प्रोटोकॉल राज्यक गरिमा सुरक्षित राखि सकैत अछि, मुदा नेपालक राष्ट्रहितक रक्षा लेल दुनू मे सँ कोनो पर्याप्त नहि अछि । अतः विदेश मंत्री शिसिर खनाल केरऽ भारत आरू चीन केरऽ यात्रा केरऽ अंदाजा खाली ई बात स॑ नै करलऽ जाय कि हुनकऽ स्वागत केकरा स॑ करलऽ गेलै, हुनकऽ कतेक गर्मजोशी स॑ स्वागत करलऽ गेलै, या कोन-कोन बयान जारी करलऽ गेलै । नेपाल प्रस्थानसँ पहिने की तैयार केलक, टेबुल पर की राखल, आ घुरलाक बाद की फॉलोअप केलक, एहिसँ हुनका लोकनिक आकलन करबाक चाही ।

ई दौरा ऐन्हऽ समय म॑ ऐलै जब॑ राष्ट्रीय स्वतंत्र पार्टी (आरएसपी) के नेतृत्व वाला नयका सरकार अपनऽ विदेश-नीति शैली क॑ परिभाषित करै, दोनों पड़ोसी क॑ आश्वस्त करै आरू शुरुआती कूटनीतिक विवादऽ स॑ आगू बढ़ै के कोशिश करी रहलऽ छै । सवाल ई नहि अछि जे भ्रमण जरूरी छल कि नहि। ओ सभ छलाह। एहि स बेसी महत्वपूर्ण सवाल इ अछि जे की नेपाल संरचित एजेंडा क संग गेल आ मापबा योग्य अनुवर्ती पालन क संग वापस आबि गेल।

संबंधित लेख स्प्लर्जिंग बंद करू, स्नोबॉलिंग शुरू करू : मार्शमैलो इफेक्ट सं हॉकी स्टिक इफेक्ट तक के यात्रा विश्व कप में ग्रुप प्ले के अंतिम दिन के राउंड 32 के मैदान तैयार करैत अछि

नेपाल लेल दुनू पड़ोसी स जुड़ाव अपरिहार्य अछि। मुदा नेपाल मे कूटनीतिक यात्राक अभाव नहि अछि । एकरा म॑ जे कमी छै, वू छै जिम्मेदारी, समय सीमा आरू वू यात्रा के बाद नापलऽ जाय वाला कार्रवाई के दृश्यमान प्रणाली ।

ई खास करी क॑ वू सरकार लेली मायने रखै छै जे पुरानऽ राजनीति स॑ टूटै के वादा करी क॑ सत्ता म॑ ऐलै । प्रधानमंत्री बलेन्द्र शाह अधिक नियंत्रित कूटनीतिक शैली शुरू करै लेली दृढ़ संकल्पित नजर आबी गेलऽ छै, जेकरा म॑ प्रोटोकॉल, संस्थागत चैनल, आरू विदेशी राजनयिकऽ के साथ राज्य के नेतृत्व म॑ जुड़ाव प॑ अधिक जोर देलऽ गेलऽ छै । सचमुच नेपाल के प्रोफेशनल कूटनीतिक संस्कृति के जरूरत छै। राज्य सं राज्य संबंधक कें संचालन संस्थाक, रिकॉर्ड, प्रोटोकॉल, आ राष्ट्रीय प्राथमिकता कें माध्यम सं करनाय आवश्यक छै.

गूगलटैग.सीएमडी.पुश (फंक्शन () { गूगलटैग.प्रदर्शन ('डिव-जीपीटी-विज्ञापन-1686127029429-0'); }); गूगलटैग.सीएमडी.पुश (फंक्शन () { गूगलटैग.प्रदर्शन ('डिव-जीपीटी-विज्ञापन-1686127067910-0'); }); गूगलटैग.सीएमडी.पुश (फंक्शन () { गूगलटैग.प्रदर्शन ('डिव-जीपीटी-विज्ञापन-1686127112906-0'); });

मुदा प्रोटोकॉल असगर कूटनीति नहि अछि। प्रोटोकॉल गरिमा के रक्षा क सकैत अछि, मुदा संरचित एजेंडा मात्र राष्ट्रीय हित के रक्षा करैत अछि । भ्रमणक समय एहि बात केँ आओर स्पष्ट क' देलक। हुनका सब स पहिने सरकार पहिनहि स कूटनीतिक जांच मे छल। नेपाल-भारत सीमा मुद्दा पर प्रधानमंत्रीक रिपोर्ट कएल गेल टिप्पणी अतिक्रमण आ ब्रिटिश युगक ऐतिहासिक अभिलेखक संभावित भूमिका पर बहस सहित विवाद उत्पन्न केलक । बाद मे स्पष्टीकरण मे कहल गेल अछि जे नेपाल थर्ड पार्टी मध्यस्थता नहि मांगि रहल अछि। मुदा ताबत धरि एहि टिप्पणी स नेपालक भीतर बहस शुरू भ गेल छल आ भारत दिस स थर्ड पार्टी क संलग्नता कए खारिज करैत प्रतिक्रिया सेहो शुरू भ गेल छल।

ई पृष्ठभूमि म॑ आरएसपी अध्यक्ष रबी लामिछाने केरऽ भारत यात्रा, जेकरा बाद विदेश मंत्री केरऽ भारत आरू चीन केरऽ आधिकारिक यात्रा के गहन आकलन के हकदार छै ।

भारत यात्रा स॑ भारत क॑ ई बात के आश्वस्त करै के मौका मिललै कि नेपाल केरऽ नया राजनीतिक शैली जिम्मेदार राज्यशिल्प के भीतर ही रहतै, जबकि बातचीत क॑ व्यापार, निवेश, संपर्क, ऊर्जा, आरू जन-जन संबंध जैसनऽ व्यावहारिक क्षेत्रऽ के तरफ स्थानांतरित करी देलकै । चीन यात्रा सेहो मायने रखैत छल।

भारत के साथ दृश्यमान जुड़ाव के बाद नेपाल के उत्तरी पड़ोसी के निरंतरता, संतुलन, आरू सम्मान के संप्रेषण लेली बीजिंग तक पहुँचना जरूरी छेलै । एहि मे बुनियादी ढांचा, कनेक्टिविटी, व्यापार, निवेश, पर्यटन, कृषि, ऊर्जा, प्रौद्योगिकी, डिजिटलीकरण, आ पिछला समझौता क क्रियान्वयन सहित व्यापक क्षेत्र शामिल छल ।

दुनू यात्राक मूल्य छल। चैनल खुजल रखलक, अनिश्चितता कम केलक, आ नव सरकारक बाहरी मुद्रा शुरू केलक। लेकिन केंद्रीय सवाल ई छै कि की ई सब पर्याप्त संरचित आरू आपसी समझलऽ गेलऽ एजेंडा स॑ निर्देशित छेलै, या ई उपयोगी लेकिन व्यापक रूप स॑ फ्रेम करलऽ गेलऽ कूटनीतिक जुड़ाव छेलै जेकरा म॑ अनुवर्ती केरऽ सीमित सार्वजनिक सबूत छेलै ?

सार्वजनिक रिकॉर्ड मे व्यापक एजेंडा, चुनल गेल आउटपुट, आ सामान्य प्रतिबद्धताक कें दर्शाओल गेल छै. भारत लेली कहलऽ गेलऽ क्षेत्रऽ म॑ व्यापार, निवेश, कनेक्टिविटी, ऊर्जा, आरू जन-जन संबंध शामिल छेलै । बाद केरऽ टिप्पणी म॑ डिजिटल सहयोग, सीमा पार स॑ भुगतान प्रणाली, शैक्षिक साझेदारी, उभरतऽ तकनीक, आर्टिफिशियल इंटेलिजेंस, आरू क्षेत्रीय भाषा प्रौद्योगिकी के जिक्र करलऽ गेलऽ छेलै । चीन लेली सार्वजनिक रिकॉर्ड म॑ बुनियादी ढाँचा, कनेक्टिविटी, सीमा प्रबंधन, ऊर्जा, व्यापार, निवेश, कृषि, प्रौद्योगिकी हस्तांतरण, डिजिटलीकरण, पर्यटन आरू जन-जन संबंधऽ के पार सहयोग के साथ-साथ पिछला कार्यक्रम आरू परियोजना क॑ लागू करै के प्रतिबद्धता के जिक्र करलऽ गेलऽ छेलै ।

ई सब महत्वपूर्ण अछि। लेकिन व्यापक विषय, चुनल गेल आउटपुट आ सामान्य प्रतिबद्धता सार्वजनिक रूप सं खुलासा कैल गेल अनुवर्ती योजना कें समान नहि छै. उपलब्ध सार्वजनिक रिकॉर्ड अखन तइक जिम्मेदार एजेंसियॉ, समकक्ष संस्थाक, समय सीमा, आ मापल जा सकय वाला अगिला चरणक कें पहचान करय वाला कोनों यात्रा कें लेल संरचित कार्य मैट्रिक्स नहि देखायत छै. ठीक ओहि अंतर के कारण नेपाल के एजेंडा नेतृत्व वाला कूटनीति के जरूरत अछि.

गंभीर उच्च स्तरीय कूटनीतिक यात्रा कए प्रस्थान स पहिने पांचटा सवाल क जवाब देबाक चाही। नेपाल प्रत्येक यात्रा स ठीक-ठीक की चाहैत अछि? की एजेंडा पर चर्चा भेल अछि आ दोसर पक्ष सं व्यापक रूप सं सहमत भ गेल अछि? कोन-कोन मुद्दा निर्णय, तकनीकी समीक्षा, या राजनीतिक संकेत देबय लेल तैयार अछि? वापसी के बाद के फॉलोअप करत? संसद आ नागरिक के कोना पता चलत जे एहि यात्रा के परिणाम निकलल कि नहि?

भारत के लेल व्यापक चर्चा ठोस कार्यधारा बनय पड़त : बिजली व्यापार, बाजार पहुंच, सीमा शुल्क सुविधा, डिजिटल भुगतान, सीमा बुनियादी ढांचा, हवाई मार्ग, आ भारत मे नेपाली के व्यावहारिक जरूरत. सीमा के मुद्दा के साक्ष्य, संस्था, आ अनुशासित भाषा के माध्यम सं संभालल जेबाक चाही.

चीन लेल सेहो इएह अनुशासन चाही। यदि व्यापार घाटा, पर्यटन, बुनियादी ढांचा या पूर्व समझौता उठल अछि त नेपाल के प्राथमिकता वाला कार्य के पहचान करय पड़त, व्यवहार्यता के आकलन करय पड़त, विलंब के बुझय पड़त आ जिम्मेदारी देबय पड़त.

अतः विदेश मंत्री केरऽ यात्रा स॑ एगो नया मानक शुरू होना चाहियऽ जेकरा म॑ हर बड़ऽ विदेश यात्रा म॑ दौरा के बाद के कार्रवाई मैट्रिक्स पैदा होय छै । एहन मैट्रिक्स कें संवेदनशील विवरण कें खुलासा करय कें जरूरत नहि छै. मुदा एहि मे चर्चा भेल मुद्दा, जिम्मेदार नेपाली एजेंसी, समकक्ष संस्था, अगिला डेग, समय सीमा, आ आगू वार्ता करबाक आवश्यकता वाला मामला के सूची बनाबय के चाही. कूटनीति एहि तरहेँ शासन बनि जाइत अछि ।

पुरानऽ दलऽ न॑ विदेश यात्रा भी करलकै, बयान जारी करलकै, आरू समझौता प॑ हस्ताक्षर करलकै जे अक्सर अप्रभावित रहै छेलै । आरएसपा के नेतृत्व वाला सरकार के ओहि संस्कृति के नव चेहरा सं दोहराबय के नहि चाही.

नेपाल के भारत के विश्वास के साथ, चीन के स्पष्टता के साथ, आरू दोनों पड़ोसी देश के राष्ट्रीय हित के दृष्टिकोण स॑ जोड़ै के जरूरत छै । नेपाल के कमजोर करय वाला बात दुनू पड़ोसी के संग जुड़ाव नहिं, बल्कि बिना तैयारी के जुड़ाव, बिना अनुशासन के बयान, आ बिना फॉलोअप के दौरा अछि.

नेपाल के कूटनीति के भविष्य के परीक्षा ई नै होबाक चाही जे नई दिल्ली में नेता के गर्मजोशी स स्वागत होइत अछि या बीजिंग में। परीक्षण ई होबाक चाही जे यात्रा सहमत एजेंडा पर आधारित छल, प्रतिबद्धताक पालन कयल गेल छल, आ प्रतिनिधिमंडल के वापसी के बाद नागरिक के परिणाम देखल गेल छल.

घाले जेनेवा म॑ डब्ल्यूएचओ केरऽ शासन टीम केरऽ पूर्व सदस्य छै आरू शासन, नीति, आरू कूटनीति म॑ रुचि रखै छै

title: Nepal has to plan beyond megawatts - Nepali Times
description: Nepal has to plan beyond megawatts  Nepali Times
publisher.title: Nepali Times
full_article:
For close to a decade, my work has taken me to the places where hydropower actually gets built, walking to huge concrete structures in a deep river valley.

I have argued with site managers about hard hats no inspector will ever check, spending afternoons with community leaders who want to know what happened to the fish. From those construction sites, the national conversation looks oddly narrow.

The story at conferences and seminars in Kathmandu is almost entirely about megawatts. It is a good and uplifting story, but it is also incomplete.

If Nepal means to be South Asia’s source of clean energy, what is sold across the border must carry more than electrons. It must also carry credible proof that the electricity was generated in a way that the buyer, the lender and the family displaced by the dam can all defend.

By March 2026, Nepal’s installed capacity topped 4,105MW, almost all of it hydro. It was only 700MW fifteen years ago. The grid now reaches nearly the entire population of Nepal.

Last year, the country earned $130 million from power exports to India and Bangladesh and, for the first time, Nepal sold more electricity than it bought.

But these accomplishments hide the wide seasonal variation in generation capacity. Nearly 90% of the electricity is generated from run-of-river plants where output depends on the water flow of rivers. In the monsoon Nepal now has a surplus of 1,200MW, while in winter there is still a deficit that has to be covered with imports from India.

The country is therefore a net annual exporter but is structurally import-dependent. The boast and the vulnerability are derived from the seasonal variation in power output. This is the strongest argument that Nepal’s next phase in energy development has to come from storage schemes.

The Energy Development Roadmap sets the target of 28,500MW by 2035. This year’s budget allocated $640 million for generation, transmission and distribution, and a plan to add 670MW of hydro and 370MW of solar this year alone. This is a clear admission that the dry-season gap needs move beyond run-of-river plants.

However, the project development documents for hydropower are limited to the EIA or IEE rituals and do not mention cumulative impact on the ecology and communities in the valleys.

CLIMATE RISK

Then there is the overarching risk of climate breakdown. The same warming that makes Nepal’s clean power valuable to its neighbours is destabilising the rivers that the power depends on.

The Himalaya is heating at roughly twice the global rate and run-of-river schemes are affected directly. The hydropower plants have been built directly downstream from 47 potentially dangerous glacial lakes.

In 2016, a glacial lake outburst flood swept away the 45MW Upper Bhotekosi plant; in July 2025 a glacier collapse in China knocked out roughly 200MW in one morning, including the 111MW Rasuwagadi plant plant commissioned only a year eaerlier.

New and well-built is not the same as safe. Addressing climate risk can no longer be an afterthought, it has to be engineered into the asset and assessed across the whole basin, because a flood has never respected a project boundary.

The change I see on the ground is real, if uneven. Developers that a decade ago had no one on staff to answer for the river are hiring their first environmental and social professionals and writing E&S policies that sit above any single project.

Community engagement is shifting from a signature-gathering formality towards genuine consultation. International investors have funded a basin-wide cumulative impact assessment of the Trisuli, the kind of whole-river study that our regulators can take a lead on for all basins.

And engineers are beginning to design fish passes built for the species actually in the river, a route the snow trout will use rather than a concrete staircase that only looks right on a drawing. The momentum is unmistakable, but it is still felt far less across the regulatory and private mainstream.

Three changes are needed in order to go beyond megawatts;

Regulation must be proportionate to risk. The core parameters have barely moved in decades. The environmental-flow approach traces back to the 2001 hydropower policy and the EIA framework to the early 1990s and they are applied almost the same way to every project and the same checklist for a 6 MW plant in a quiet valley as for a 900 MW reservoir that displaces thousands. That is not a system, it is a formality. Climate-proof the infrastructure itself and strengthen every project to be engineered and stress-tested against glacial-lake outburst floods and shifting river flows, and fund upstream monitoring and cross-border early-warning systems. Require a basin-level cumulative impact assessment before licensing on the Kosi, Karnali, Gandaki and Mahakali, held by a body with the authority.

The domestic story cannot be lost inside the export one. Traditional biomass such as firewood, dung, and crop residue still makes up the bulk of the energy Nepal actually burn. Clean electricity is a small fraction of total consumption, and most kitchens are not yet electrified.

We have to build for the region and electrify our own homes at the same time, not in sequence. Nepal’s neighbours are not only buying electricity — India’s decarbonisation and Bangladesh’s energy security both depend on the power they can defend to their own regulators and voters. That assurance is the product; the megawatts are merely its unit of measure.

People sometimes ask whether responsible business is a luxury that least-developed countries can afford. The answer sits in the project files. The schemes that failed did not do so from spending too much on safeguards, they failed from spending too little, and then paid for it twice.

None of the tools required is mysterious or beyond a country at our stage. International standards exist, the basin studies can be done, the partnership models are already ours. What is missing is the discipline to treat them as the rule rather than the exception, and the will to write that discipline into the next licence, the next financing, the next budget line.

None of it needs new money but requires sequencing, putting the safeguards in front of the megawatts, not behind them. Get that right, and Nepal will not just be exporting electricity it will be offering the region something rarer and far harder to copy — a model of development with a conscience.
translated:
करीब एक दशक स हमर काज हमरा ओहि ठाम ल गेल अछि जतय वास्तव मे जलविद्युत बनैत अछि, गहींर नदी घाटी मे विशाल कंक्रीट क संरचना तक पैदल जाइत छी।

हम साइट मैनेजर सब सं हार्ड हैट पर बहस केने छी जे कोनो इंस्पेक्टर कहियो चेक नहिं करत, दुपहर में समुदाय के नेता सब सं बिताबैत छी जे जानय चाहैत छथि जे माछ के की भेल. ओहि निर्माण स्थल सब सं राष्ट्रीय गप्प-सप्प अजीब संकीर्ण बुझाइत अछि.

काठमांडू मे सम्मेलन आ गोष्ठी मे कथा लगभग पूर्ण रूप स मेगावाट क अछि। नीक आ उत्थानकारी कथा अछि, मुदा अपूर्ण सेहो अछि ।

अगर नेपाल के मतलब दक्षिण एशिया के स्वच्छ ऊर्जा के स्रोत बनय के अछि त सीमा पार जे चीज बिकाइत अछि ओकरा इलेक्ट्रॉन सं बेसि ल’ जेबाक चाही. एकरा म॑ ई बात के विश्वसनीय सबूत भी होना चाहियऽ कि बिजली ऐन्हऽ तरीका स॑ पैदा करलऽ गेलऽ छेलै जेकरऽ रक्षा खरीदार, उधारदाता आरू बांध स॑ विस्थापित परिवार सब करी सकै छै ।

मार्च 2026 तक नेपाल के स्थापित क्षमता 4,105 मेगावाट के पार भ गेल छल, जे लगभग सबटा हाइड्रो. पन्द्रह साल पहिने मात्र 700 मेगावाट क बिजली छल। आब ई ग्रिड नेपालक लगभग पूरा आबादी धरि पहुँचि गेल अछि ।

पिछला साल भारत आ बांग्लादेश कए बिजली निर्यात स देश कए 13 करोड़ डॉलर क कमाई भेल आ पहिल बेर नेपाल खरीदल गेल बिजली स बेसी बिजली बेचलक।

लेकिन ई उपलब्धि सब उत्पादन क्षमता म॑ मौसमी व्यापक भिन्नता क॑ छिपाबै छै । लगभग 90% बिजली रन-ऑफ-रिवर प्लांट सं बनय छै जतय उत्पादन नदीक कें पानी कें प्रवाह पर निर्भर करय छै. मानसून मे नेपाल मे आब 1200 मेगावाट के अधिशेष अछि, जखन कि जाड़ मे एखनो घाटा अछि जेकरा भारत सं आयात सं पूरा करय पड़ैत अछि.

अइ लेल देश शुद्ध वार्षिक निर्यातक छै मुदा संरचनात्मक रूप सं आयात पर निर्भर छै. घमंड आ भेद्यता बिजली उत्पादन मे मौसमी भिन्नता स निकलल अछि। ई सबस मजबूत तर्क अछि जे ऊर्जा विकास मे नेपाल क अगिला चरण भंडारण योजना स आबय पड़त।

ऊर्जा विकास रोडमैप मे 2035 तक 28,500 मेगावाट क लक्ष्य निर्धारित कैल गेल अछि।एहि बेर क बजट मे उत्पादन, संचरण आ वितरण क लेल 64 करोड़ डॉलर क आवंटन कैल गेल अछि, आओर एहि साल मात्र 670 मेगावाट जल आ 370 मेगावाट सौर ऊर्जा कए जोड़बाक योजना अछि। ई स्पष्ट स्वीकृति छै कि शुष्क मौसम के अंतर क॑ रन-ऑफ-रिवर पौधा स॑ आगू बढ़ै के जरूरत छै ।

लेकिन जलविद्युत कें लेल परियोजना विकास दस्तावेज ईआईए या आईईई संस्कारक कें लेल सीमित छै आ घाटी मे पारिस्थितिकी आ समुदायक पर संचयी प्रभाव कें जिक्र नहि छै.

जलवायु जोखिम

तखन जलवायु टूटबाक सर्वांगीण जोखिम सेहो अछि । वही वार्मिंग जे नेपाल केरऽ स्वच्छ शक्ति क॑ अपनऽ पड़ोसी देशऽ लेली मूल्यवान बनाबै छै, वू नदी सब क॑ अस्थिर करी रहलऽ छै, जेकरा प॑ बिजली निर्भर छै ।

हिमालय वैश्विक दर स॑ लगभग दुगुना दर स॑ गरम होय रहलऽ छै आरू रन-ऑफ-रिवर योजना प॑ एकरऽ सीधा असर पड़ै छै । जलविद्युत संयंत्र 47 संभावित खतरनाक हिमनदी झील सं सीधा नीचा बनाओल गेल अछि.

2016 में हिमनदी झील में फटला सं बाढ़ि सं 45 मेगावाट के अपर भोटेकोसी संयंत्र बह गेल छल; जुलाई २०२५ म॑ चीन म॑ ग्लेशियर केरऽ गिरला स॑ एक सुबह म॑ लगभग २०० मेगावाट के बिजली गिरी गेलै, जेकरा म॑ १११ मेगावाट के रसुवागाडी प्लांट प्लांट भी शामिल छेलै जे केवल एक साल पहलें चालू करलऽ गेलऽ छेलै ।

नव आ नीक बनल आ सुरक्षित एके नहि। जलवायु जोखिम कें संबोधित करनाय आब एकटा आफ्टर थॉट नहि भ सकय छै, एकरा संपत्ति मे इंजीनियरिंग करय पड़तय आ पूरा बेसिन मे ओकर आकलन करय पड़तय, कियाकि बाढ़ सं कहियो कोनों परियोजना सीमा कें सम्मान नै कैल गेल छै.

जमीन पर जे परिवर्तन देखैत छी से वास्तविक अछि, जँ असमान अछि। जे डेवलपर एक दशक पहिने एहि नदी क जवाब देबा लेल स्टाफ मे कियो नहि छल, ओ अपन पहिल पर्यावरण आ सामाजिक पेशेवर कए राखि रहल छथि आ ई एंड एस नीति लिखि रहल छथि जे कोनो एक परियोजना स ऊपर बैसल अछि।

सामुदायिक जुड़ाव हस्ताक्षर जुटाबै के औपचारिकता स॑ वास्तविक परामर्श के तरफ बढ़ी रहलऽ छै । अंतर्राष्ट्रीय निवेशक न॑ त्रिसुली केरऽ बेसिन-व्यापी संचयी प्रभाव आकलन केरऽ वित्तपोषण करल॑ छै, जे तरह के पूरा नदी केरऽ अध्ययन छै जेकरा प॑ हमरऽ नियामक सब बेसिन लेली अगुवाई करी सकै छै ।

आरू इंजीनियर सब वास्तव म॑ नदी म॑ मौजूद प्रजाति लेली बनलऽ फिश पास के डिजाइन बनाबै लगलऽ छै, जेकरा प॑ स्नो ट्राउट कंक्रीट के सीढ़ी के बजाय इस्तेमाल करतै जे केवल ड्राइंग प॑ सही नजर आबै छै । गति अचूक छै, लेकिन नियामक आरू निजी मुख्यधारा म॑ अखनी भी ई कहीं कम महसूस होय रहलऽ छै ।

मेगावाट सं आगू बढ़य लेल तीन टा बदलाव के जरूरत अछि;

नियमन जोखिम कें आनुपातिक होबाक चाही. दशकों मे कोर पैरामीटर मुश्किल स आगू बढ़ल अछि। पर्यावरण-प्रवाह दृष्टिकोण 2001 कें जलविद्युत नीति आ ईआईए ढाँचा सं 1990 कें दशक कें शुरु आत सं शुरू होयत छै आ इ हर परियोजना पर लगभग ओय तरह सं लागू कैल जायत छै आ शांत घाटी मे 6 मेगावाट कें संयंत्र कें लेल ओय तरह कें चेकलिस्ट कें रूप मे लागू कैल जायत छै जेना 900 मेगावाट कें जलाशय कें लेल जे हजारक कें विस्थापित करयत छै. जे कोनो व्यवस्था नहि, औपचारिकता थिक। खुद बुनियादी ढाँचा कें जलवायु-प्रूफ करनाय आ हर परियोजना कें मजबूत करनाय जेकरा हिमनदी-झील कें प्रकोप बाढ़ आ बदलैत नदी कें प्रवाह कें खिलाफ इंजीनियरिंग आ तनाव-परीक्षण कैल जै, आ अपस्ट्रीम निगरानी आ सीमा पार सं पूर्व चेतावनी प्रणाली कें लेल फंडिंग करनाय. कोसी, कर्णाली, गंडकी आ महाकाली पर लाइसेंस देय सं पहिने बेसिन स्तरीय संचयी प्रभाव आकलन कें आवश्यकता होयत छै, जे प्राधिकारी कें संग कोनों निकाय कें पास छै.

निर्यात वाला के भीतर घरेलू कहानी नै हेराय सकै छै। पारंपरिक बायोमास जेना जलाऊ लकड़ी, गोबर, आ फसल कें अवशेष एखनहु नेपाल वास्तव मे जलाबै वाला ऊर्जा कें थोक कें हिस्सा बनय छै. स्वच्छ बिजली कुल खपत कें एकटा छोट हिस्सा छै, आ अधिकतर भनसा घरक मे एखन धरि बिजली नहि देल गेल छै.

हमरा सब के क्षेत्र के लेल निर्माण करय पड़त आ एकहि संग अपन घर के विद्युतीकरण करय पड़त, क्रम में नहि। नेपाल केरऽ पड़ोसी देश खाली बिजली ही नै खरीदी रहलऽ छै — भारत केरऽ डिकार्बनीकरण आरू बांग्लादेश केरऽ ऊर्जा सुरक्षा दोनों ही वू शक्ति प॑ निर्भर करै छै जेकरा वू अपनऽ नियामक आरू मतदाता के सामने बचाबै सकै छै । ओ आश्वासन उत्पाद थिक; मेगावाट मात्र ओकर माप इकाई अछि ।

लोक कखनो काल पूछैत अछि जे जिम्मेदार कारोबार कोनो एहन विलासिता अछि जे कम विकसित देश खरीद सकैत अछि। जवाब प्रोजेक्ट फाइल मे बैसल अछि। जे योजना असफल भेल से सुरक्षा पर बेसी खर्च करबा स नहि भेल, बेसी कम खर्च करबा स असफल भ गेल, आ फेर दू बेर एकर भुगतान केलक।

जे कोनो औजार चाही से रहस्यमयी या हमरा सबहक मंच पर कोनो देश स परे नहि अछि। अंतर्राष्ट्रीय मानक मौजूद अछि, बेसिन के अध्ययन भ सकैत अछि, साझेदारी के मॉडल पहिने सं हमर अछि. जे कमी छै, वू छै ओकरा अपवाद के बजाय नियम के रूप म॑ देखै के अनुशासन, आरू वू अनुशासन क॑ अगला लाइसेंस, अगला फाइनेंसिंग, अगला बजट लाइन म॑ लिखै के इच्छाशक्ति ।

एकरा म॑ स॑ कोय भी नया पैसा के जरूरत नै छै लेकिन सीक्वेंसिंग के जरूरत छै, सुरक्षा क॑ मेगावाट के सामने नै, पाछू नै रखना । से अधिकार प्राप्त करू, आ नेपाल खाली बिजली निर्यात नै करत ई क्षेत्र के किछु दुर्लभ आ नकल करब कहीं कठिन पेशकश करत — विवेक के संग विकास के मॉडल।

title: Video | Nepal Protests | Why Nepal Gen-Z Is Protesting Against Balen Shah Government - NDTV
description: Video | Nepal Protests | Why Nepal Gen-Z Is Protesting Against Balen Shah Government  NDTV
publisher.title: NDTV
full_article: Hundreds of people came out on the roads in Nepal's Kathmandu today in protest against the government's move to evict squatters without a resettlement plan. This is the first major challenge against the government led by Prime Minister Balen Shah, who had supported the fierce Gen-Z protest last year that ended the reign of KP Sharma Oli and his team. The protest that began against the Kathmandu Metropolitan City Police has been going on for some days. It escalated sharply after many young people criticised the government for evicting and relocating landless people to holding centres. The latest protest, which has a distinct Gen-Z character, highlighted the "inhuman" living conditions of the evicted people. Local reports said some activists, students and journalists have been arrested for raising the issues of people forced to live at the holding centres.
translated: नेपाल के काठमांडू मे बिना पुनर्वास योजना के सुकून के लोक के बेदखल करय के सरकार के कदम के विरोध मे आई सैकड़ों लोक सड़क पर निकलल छथिन्ह. प्रधानमंत्री बैलेन शाह के नेतृत्व वाला सरकार के खिलाफ ई पहिलौ बड़ौ चुनौती छेकै, जे पिछला साल जेन-जेड के भयंकर विरोध प्रदर्शन के समर्थन करलकै जेकरा स॑ केपी शर्मा ओली आरू हुनकऽ टीम के शासन के अंत होय गेलै । काठमांडू महानगरपालिका प्रहरी के विरुद्ध शुरु भेल विरोध प्रदर्शन किछु दिन स चलि रहल अछि । भूमिहीन लोक के बेदखल आ होल्डिंग सेंटर में स्थानांतरित करय के लेल बहुतो युवा के सरकार के आलोचना के बाद ई तेजी सं बढ़ल. ताजा विरोध प्रदर्शन, जेकरऽ एगो अलग जेन-जेड चरित्र छै, बेदखल लोगऽ के "अमानवीय" जीवन केरऽ स्थिति प॑ प्रकाश डाललकै । स्थानीय रिपोर्ट मे कहल गेल अछि जे होल्डिंग सेंटर मे रहय लेल मजबूर लोक के मुद्दा उठाबय के मामला मे किछ कार्यकर्ता, छात्र आओर पत्रकार के गिरफ्तार कएल गेल अछि.

title: Nepal Geotechnical Society wins top global engineering award - The Kathmandu Post
description: Nepal Geotechnical Society wins top global engineering award  The Kathmandu Post
publisher.title: The Kathmandu Post
full_article:
The Nepal Geotechnical Society (NGS) has received the ISSMGE Outstanding Member Society Award 2026, one of the highest honours conferred by the International Society for Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering (ISSMGE). Australia was also named a recipient of this year's award.



To celebrate the achievement, the society organised a special felicitation and commemorative programme at the Universal Engineering and Science College in Lalitpur on Sunday.

The ISSMGE, a global professional organisation representing national geotechnical societies from 96 countries, presents the Outstanding Member Society Award every four years to recognise national member organisations that have made exceptional contributions to the advancement of geotechnical engineering and to the international geotechnical community.

According to engineer Kalpana Adhikari, NGS general secretary, Nepal received the award in recognition of its contributions to the development of professional geotechnical engineering, international collaboration, knowledge sharing and the society's active institutional engagement.

Speaking at the event, Adhikari shared her experience of accepting the award on behalf of Nepal at the 21st International Conference on Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering, held in Vienna, Austria. She described the opportunity to represent Nepal on an international platform as a matter of great pride.

The celebration was attended by representatives of various engineering professional organisations, government officials, university professors, representatives from the private sector, senior geotechnical engineers, and office-bearers and members of the Nepal Geotechnical Society.

The ISSMGE president, immediate past president and vice president for Asia also joined the programme virtually to congratulate Nepal. They praised the country's growing role and contribution within the international geotechnical engineering community.

NGS president Indra Prasad Acharya said the recognition was the result of the collective efforts of the society's past executive committees, former presidents, government agencies, universities, the private sector and partner professional organisations. He thanked all those who had contributed to the society's progress, saying the award belonged to everyone who had supported its journey.
translated:
नेपाल भू-तकनीकी सोसायटी (एनजीएस) क॑ इन्टरनेशनल सोयल मैकेनिक्स एंड जियोटेक्निकल इंजीनियरिंग सोसाइटी (ISSMGE) द्वारा देलऽ जाय वाला सर्वोच्च सम्मानऽ म॑ स॑ एक आईएसएसएमजीई उत्कृष्ट सदस्य समाज पुरस्कार २०२६ मिललऽ छै । ऑस्ट्रेलिया क॑ भी ई साल केरऽ पुरस्कार केरऽ प्राप्तकर्ता के रूप म॑ चुनलऽ गेलै ।



एहि उपलब्धि के जश्न मनाबय लेल समाज के तरफ सं रविदिन ललितपुर के यूनिवर्सल इंजीनियरिंग एंड साइंस कॉलेज मे विशेष अभिनंदन आ स्मरण कार्यक्रम के आयोजन कायल गेल.

96 देशक कें राष्ट्रीय भू-तकनीकी समाजक कें प्रतिनिधित्व करय वाला वैश्विक पेशेवर संगठन आईएसएसएमजीई हर चारि साल पर उत्कृष्ट सदस्य समाज पुरस्कार प्रदान करयत छै, जेकरा सं राष्ट्रीय सदस्य संगठनक कें पहचान कैल जायत छै जे भू-तकनीकी इंजीनियरिंग कें उन्नति मे आ अंतर्राष्ट्रीय भू-तकनीकी समुदाय मे असाधारण योगदान देलक छै.

एनजीएस के महासचिव अभियंता कल्पना अधिकारी के अनुसार नेपाल के ई पुरस्कार पेशेवर भू-तकनीकी अभियांत्रिकी के विकास, अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सहयोग, ज्ञान साझा करना आरू समाज के सक्रिय संस्थागत संलग्नता के सम्मान में मिललै ।

एहि आयोजन मे अधिकारी आस्ट्रियाक वियना मे आयोजित मृदा यांत्रिकी आ भू-तकनीकी अभियांत्रिकी विषयक २१म अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय सम्मेलन मे नेपालक दिस सँ पुरस्कार स्वीकार करबाक अनुभव बतौलनि । ओ अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय मंच पर नेपालक प्रतिनिधित्व करबाक अवसर केँ बहुत गौरवक विषय बतौलनि ।

उत्सवमे विभिन्न अभियांत्रिकी व्यावसायिक संस्थाक प्रतिनिधि, सरकारी अधिकारी, विश्वविद्यालयक प्राध्यापक, निजी क्षेत्रक प्रतिनिधि, वरिष्ठ भू-तकनीकी अभियंता, आ नेपाल भू-तकनीकी सोसाइटीके पदाधिकारी आ सदस्यसब सहभागिता रहल छल ।

आईएसएसएमजीई के अध्यक्ष, तत्काल पूर्व अध्यक्ष आ एशिया के उपाध्यक्ष सेहो नेपाल के बधाई देबय लेल वर्चुअल रूप सं कार्यक्रम मे शामिल भेलाह. अंतरराष्ट्रीय भू-तकनीकी इंजीनियरिंग समुदाय के भीतर देश के बढ़तऽ भूमिका आरू योगदान के प्रशंसा करलकै ।

एनजीएस के अध्यक्ष इंद्रप्रसाद आचार्य कहलनि जे ई मान्यता समाज के विगत कार्यकारिणी, पूर्व अध्यक्ष, सरकारी एजेंसी, विश्वविद्यालय, निजी क्षेत्र आ साझेदार पेशेवर संगठन के सामूहिक प्रयास के परिणाम अछि। समाज के प्रगति में योगदान देनिहार सब गोटे के धन्यवाद दैत कहलनि जे ई पुरस्कार ओकर यात्रा में सहयोग करय वाला सब गोटे के अछि।

full_article: ...EXTREME HEAT WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 AM MDT TUESDAY... * WHAT...Dangerously hot conditions with temperatures up to 107 possible for the valleys of northern Utah, including the Wasatch Front and Cache Valley. Highs around 100 possible for the mountain valleys of northern and central Utah, including the Wasatch Back, along Castle Country, the Uinta Basin, and the San Rafael Swell. * WHERE...Portions of central and northern Utah. * WHEN...Until 6 AM MDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Heat related illnesses increase significantly during extreme heat events. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...There is high confidence that temperatures will warm to values challenging daily, monthly, or even all-time high temperature records Sunday through Monday. Overnight lows in the lower to mid 70s for many valley locations will provide little in the way of overnight relief. PRECAUTIONARY/PREPAREDNESS ACTIONS... Drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, stay out of the sun, and check up on relatives and neighbors. Do not leave young children and pets in unattended vehicles. Car interiors will reach lethal temperatures in a matter of minutes. Take extra precautions when outside. Wear lightweight and loose fitting clothing. Try to limit strenuous activities to early morning or evening. Take action when you see symptoms of heat exhaustion and heat stroke. To reduce risk during outdoor work, the Occupational Safety and Health Administration recommends scheduling frequent rest breaks in shaded or air conditioned environments. Anyone overcome by heat should be moved to a cool and shaded location. Heat stroke is an emergency! Call 9 1 1. &&

title: Nepal's Tilagufa Municipality introduces mandatory apple export permits - FreshPlaza
description: Nepal's Tilagufa Municipality introduces mandatory apple export permits  FreshPlaza
publisher.title: FreshPlaza
full_article:
Tilagufa Municipality in Kalikot, Nepal, has made a local-level official recommendation mandatory for all farmers and businessmen seeking to export apples from the municipality to other districts, introducing a three-stage process to govern quality certification and export management of local produce.

Chief Administrative Officer Bishnu Prasad Kafle says farmers and businessmen must follow a set sequence before transporting apples. "To systematize the export process, the municipality has set a three-stage process, which farmers and businessmen must compulsorily follow. Farmers or businessmen must submit an application to the municipality's agricultural service center at least three days before transporting and exporting apples, clearly stating the quantity of apples and the export destination."

After the application is registered, the municipality's agricultural technicians conduct field-level monitoring and inspection of the apples, covering quality, quantity, and the condition of diseases or pests. Kafle adds: "Only after submitting the prescribed revenue according to the rules, based on the report prepared by the technical team, an official export recommendation letter will be provided from the office. This arrangement will help to make the market management of apples, which has become the identity of Tilagufa, more systematic, qualitative, and professional."

Agriculture Development Officer Govinda Bhatta says the policy responds directly to Tilagufa's role as the major apple-producing area of Kalikot. "More than 10,000 quintals of apples are exported annually from the municipality. Due to haphazard exports, the quality and image of Tilagufa's apples could be affected in the market; hence, the provision of mandatory recommendations has been made to maintain quality."
translated:
नेपालक कालीकोट स्थित तिलागुफा नगरपालिका नगरपालिकासँ अन्य जिल्लामे सेब निर्यात करय चाहय बला सभ किसान आ व्यवसायीके लेल स्थानीय तहके आधिकारिक अनुशंसा अनिवार्य कएने अछि, जाहिमे स्थानीय उपजके गुणवत्ता प्रमाणीकरण आ निर्यात प्रबंधनके संचालनके लेल तीन चरणक प्रक्रिया शुरू कएल गेल अछि ।

मुख्य प्रशासनिक अधिकारी बिष्णु प्रसाद काफ्ले के कहनाय छनि जे किसान आओर कारोबारी के सेब के परिवहन सं पहिने एकटा निर्धारित क्रम के पालन करय पड़त. "निर्यात प्रक्रिया के व्यवस्थित करय लेल नगरपालिका तीन चरण के प्रक्रिया तय केलक अछि, जेकर पालन किसान आ व्यवसायी के अनिवार्य रूप सं करय पड़त. किसान या व्यवसायी के सेब के परिवहन आ निर्यात सं कम सं कम तीन दिन पहिने नगरपालिका के कृषि सेवा केंद्र मे आवेदन जमा करय पड़त, जाहि मे सेब के मात्रा आ निर्यात के गंतव्य के स्पष्ट रूप सं बताबय पड़त."

आवेदन पंजीकृत करला के बाद नगरपालिका के कृषि तकनीशियन सेब के खेत स्तरीय निगरानी आ निरीक्षण करैत छथि, जाहि में गुणवत्ता, मात्रा, आ बीमारी या कीट के स्थिति शामिल अछि. काफ्ले आरू कहै छै, "नियम के अनुसार निर्धारित राजस्व जमा करला के बाद ही तकनीकी टीम द्वारा तैयार रिपोर्ट के आधार प कार्यालय स॑ आधिकारिक निर्यात सिफारिश पत्र उपलब्ध कराय देलऽ जैतै । ई व्यवस्था स॑ सेब के बाजार प्रबंधन, जे तिलागुफा के पहचान बनी गेलऽ छै, के बाजार प्रबंधन क॑ अधिक व्यवस्थित, गुणात्मक, आरू पेशेवर बनाबै म॑ मदद मिलतै ।"

कृषि विकास अधिकारी गोविन्द भट्ट के कहनाय छनि जे ई नीति कालीकोट के प्रमुख सेब उत्पादक क्षेत्र के रूप मे तिलागुफा के भूमिका के सीधा जवाब दैत अछि. "नगरपालिका स॑ हर साल १०,००० क्विंटल स॑ भी अधिक सेब के निर्यात होय छै । बेतरतीब निर्यात के कारण बाजार म॑ तिलागुफा केरऽ सेब के गुणवत्ता आरू छवि प्रभावित होय सकै छै; यही वजह स॑ गुणवत्ता क॑ कायम रखै लेली अनिवार्य सिफारिश के प्रावधान करलऽ गेलऽ छै ।"

title: US Citizen Arrested Near Nepal Border Eight Months After Entering India Illegally Via Sea Route - Swarajyamag
description: US Citizen Arrested Near Nepal Border Eight Months After Entering India Illegally Via Sea Route  Swarajyamag
publisher.title: Swarajyamag
full_article:
An American national has been apprehended by the Sashastra Seema Bal near the India-Nepal border in Maharajganj, Uttar Pradesh, for entering India without valid documents.

Jordan Brown, 36, who identifies as a US citizen from California, attempted to cross into Nepal on 11 July when SSB personnel intercepted him near border pillar number 516 in the Sonauli area.

During questioning, Brown revealed he had travelled to Thailand on a tourist visa, where he lost his passport. He subsequently reached Sri Lanka via a sea route and arrived in India from there by sea on 2 November 2025.

Since then, he has been residing in Goa. Brown travelled from Bengaluru to the Sonauli border with the intention of entering Nepal when border guards stopped him for verification.

The SSB recovered Rs 31,460 in cash and two mobile phones from Brown during a search. He was unable to produce a passport, visa or any other valid travel documents.

When SSB personnel stopped him for verification, Brown allegedly tried to flee but was chased and arrested by border security forces.

Authorities have registered a case against Brown at Sonauli Police Station under Sections 21/23 of the Immigration and Foreigners Act for entering India without valid documents.

Additional SP Siddharth stated that Brown's claims are currently being verified, with further legal proceedings underway.

SSB Assistant Commandant Priya Yadav confirmed that security agencies questioned him before handing him over to local police for further legal action.
translated:
उत्तर प्रदेश के महाराजगंज में भारत-नेपाल सीमा के समीप सशास्त्र सीमा बाल द्वारा बिना वैध दस्तावेज के भारत में प्रवेश करय के आरोप में एकटा अमेरिकी नागरिक के गिरफ्तार कायल गेल अछि।

कैलिफोर्निया के अमेरिकी नागरिक के रूप में पहचान करय वाला 36 वर्षीय जॉर्डन ब्राउन 11 जुलाई के नेपाल पार करय के कोशिश केने छल जखन सोनौली इलाका के सीमा खंभा नंबर 516 के पास एसएसबी के जवान हुनका रोकलक।

पूछताछ के दौरान ब्राउन न॑ खुलासा करलकै कि हुनी टूरिस्ट वीजा प॑ थाईलैंड गेलऽ छेलै, जहां हुनकऽ पासपोर्ट खतम होय गेलऽ छेलै । एकरऽ बाद समुद्री मार्ग स॑ श्रीलंका पहुँचलै आरू २ नवम्बर २०२५ क॑ वहाँ स॑ समुद्री मार्ग स॑ भारत पहुँचलै ।

तहिया स गोवा मे रहैत छथि। ब्राउन नेपाल में प्रवेश के इरादा स बेंगलुरु स सोनौली सीमा तक गेल छल, जखन कि सीमा रक्षक हुनका सत्यापन के लेल रोकलक।

एसएसबी के तलाशी के दौरान ब्राउन सं 31,460 रुपया नगद आ दू टा मोबाइल फोन बरामद भेल अछि। पासपोर्ट, वीजा या कोनो अन्य वैध यात्रा दस्तावेज पेश नहि क सकलाह।

जखन एसएसबी के जवान हुनका सत्यापन के लेल रोकलक त ब्राउन कथित तौर पर भागय के कोशिश केलक मुदा सीमा सुरक्षा बल हुनकर पीछा क गिरफ्तार क लेलक।

अधिकारि सब ब्राउन के खिलाफ सोनौली थाना में आप्रवासन आ विदेशी अधिनियम के धारा 21/23 के तहत बिना वैध दस्तावेज के भारत में प्रवेश के मामला दर्ज केलक अछि।

अतिरिक्त एसपी सिद्धार्थ के कहनाय छनि जे फिलहाल ब्राउन के दावा के सत्यापन भ रहल अछि, आओर आओर कानूनी प्रक्रिया चलि रहल अछि.

एसएसबी के सहायक कमांडेंट प्रिया यादव सुरक्षा एजेंसी हुनका आगू के कानूनी कार्रवाई के लेल स्थानीय पुलिस के सौंपय सं पहिने हुनका सं पूछताछ केने पुष्टि केलनि।

full_article:
Nepal’s rhesus macaques are raiding crops across the mid-hills. A 2026 study found nearly half their diet in one region came from cultivated crops, and farmers bearing losses largely uncompensated.

Researchers are testing AI-based detection systems, with one achieving around 88% field accuracy.

Nepal’s compensation and relocation policies have struggled to keep pace with the conflict, and a 15-member government task force formed in May 2026 has yet to report, leaving farmers to guard their fields at dawn in the meantime. See All Key Ideas

KATHMANDU — At dawn in Birta Deurali village of Kavrepalanchok in central Nepal, maize fields aren’t quiet. Farmers stand guard, scanning the trees for movement, but they aren’t the only ones there.

“If you leave even for a short time, the monkeys do considerable damage,” said 46-year-old Sagar Tamang, a resident of Birta Deurali. Villagers take turns guarding fields every two hours, beating drums and sending dogs to chase them away.

Nepal’s macaque crop raids are making national headlines, and the country’s researchers are testing artificial intelligence-based detection and deterrence systems, though even the scientists building them admit the technology isn’t yet a reliable fix.

“We even hide food indoors, but they still find their way in,” Tamang said. “Only fire scares them now,” he added, referring to the burning sticks villagers’ wave to keep the monkeys at bay.

For farmers such as Sunmaya Lama, 32, of the same village, the losses are adding up. “We lost maize worth 30,000 rupees (about $230) this year,” she said. “Over the past three years, it has reached around 90,000 rupees (about $670).”

When she approached the local government, she said she was told there was no provision for compensation. “So we just bear the loss ourselves,” she said.

A troop of rhesus macaques forage for food in Nepal. Image by Sunuwargr via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 4.0).

A brewing crisis across the country

The scale of the problem extends beyond the village. A 2022 nationwide analysis published in the Journal of Environmental Management used species distribution models to map human-rhesus macaque conflict across Nepal. It found that nearly 44% of the country’s land area contains suitable macaque habitat, with less than 8% of that within protected national parks. About 15% of all land where human settlement is permitted is characterized by moderate or high rates of conflict.

More recent research illustrated another trend. A 2026 study in the American Journal of Primatology , based on a year-long field study of a macaque troop in central Nepal, found that nearly half of their diet now comes from cultivated crops. From the moment a seed sprouts until it reaches the storage, researchers said, monkeys can damage crops at every stage.

AI enters the field

In response, researchers are testing whether technology can keep pace with the problem.

At Madan Bhandari University of Science and Technology, researcher Progress Jung Thapa is working on a system that uses AI to detect monkeys in real time and interpret their behavior. The work, carried out between 2024 and 2025, is designed for use in rural farming environments. “The monkey mind is often described as restless,” Thapa said. “But what I observed is the opposite. They are extremely present.”

The system, built on cameras and lightweight computing devices, paired a detection model with a second layer trained to interpret behavior, allowing it to classify actions such as movement, vigilance and feeding. Trained on more than 4,000 annotated images, it achieved around 88% accuracy across 28 field sessions. “If a monkey is still watching, the farmer has time,” Thapa said. “If it is already feeding, the response has to be immediate. A machine can detect. But a human decides.”

Monkeys displaying aggressive behaviors detected by the detection system designed by Progress Jung Thapa and his team. Image courtesy of Progress Jung Thapa.

The system being trained on monkeys at the Pashupatinath Temple in Kathmandu. Image courtesy of Progress Jung Thapa.

Beyond the field-accuracy figure, the underlying detection model has posted strong results: Roughly 91.7% mAP (a standard measure of detection accuracy), running at 15 to 22 frames per second on the low-cost hardware the team used. The numbers suggest the core detection technology itself is maturing quickly, even if translating that into consistent field performance remains a separate challenge.

A camera watches the field constantly, like a security camera, and a computer program checks each frame and asks, “is that a monkey?” It learned to recognize one the same way a child learns to recognize a dog by being shown thousands of photos beforehand. It also tries to guess what the monkey is doing: Watching or actively feeding. That distinction matters, since a monkey that’s just watching gives the farmer a few extra seconds, while one already eating needs an immediate response. Once the system flags a real threat, it sends an alert straight to the farmer’s phone.

At Pulchowk Campus, Institute of Engineering, Tribhuvan University, a separate group has taken a similar approach. Led by assistant professor Santosh Giri, the team has paired detection with an ultrasonic deterrent designed to repel monkeys once they are identified.

Their model was trained on a smaller dataset of fewer than 1,000 images collected from sites such as Swayambhunath and Pashupatinath in Kathmandu. While early results suggest strong detection capability, the deterrent system itself remains at a prototype stage and has yet to be tested in working farm environments.

“Agricultural environments are much more complex,” Giri said. “Lighting conditions, vegetation and partial visibility of animals can all affect performance. ”

He added that behavior poses a deeper challenge. “Rhesus macaques are highly adaptive,” he said. “Any single deterrent can lose effectiveness once animals become used to it.” To address this, his team is exploring ways to vary responses over time.

Giri’s system detects monkeys the same way, but instead of alerting a person, it fights back directly: Once a monkey is spotted, a small circuit emits a high-pitched sound above human hearing, irritating to monkeys, similar to a dog whistle, so the machine reacts instantly instead of a farmer having to run out. So far, the sound-emitting part has only been tested on a workbench, not in an actual field, so it remains unproven under real growing conditions.

Both Thapa and Giri acknowledge that translating these systems into everyday farm use has proven more difficult than early results suggested. Performance tends to drop when visibility is poor during rain, low light, or when monkeys move through dense crops or vegetation.

A 2025 systematic review of 105 studies on AI and human-wildlife conflict published in Science Progress found that while AI improved monitoring outcomes in 65% of cases, community engagement improved in only 39%, suggesting that technical performance and on-the-ground effectiveness remain distinct challenges.

Zoologist Laxman Khanal sees the core limitation clearly. “AI-based systems open new possibilities,” he said. “But if they only alert farmers, they may not be effective.”

In many villages, labor shortages already limit the ability to respond. “There are not enough people to chase monkeys,” he said. “Any system must be able to deter animals directly.”

Both Thapa and Giri said the next phase of their work is aimed directly at that gap. “In the future, AI should not just detect monkeys,” Giri said. “It should also decide how to respond.”

Experts urge caution

Naresh Subedi, a wildlife researcher, said such approaches are promising but remain uncertain. “Monkeys are highly adaptive,” he said. “If deterrents do not represent real risk, they may eventually stop responding.”

Shashank Poudel, head of wildlife programs at WWF Nepal, agreed that no single fix works alone. “A combination of methods — physical barriers, crop changes, community vigilance, and relief measures — has been more effective,” he said.

Back in Birta Deurali, this kind of caution matters more than it might in a lab. The systems Thapa and Giri are building have not yet reached villages like this one for now, guarding fields at dawn remains the only option Tamang and Lama have.

Alerts about monkeys on Slack. Image courtesy of Progress Jung Thapa.

Policy gaps and uneven responses

The problem has long been recognized at the policy level, but progress has remained uneven. Conservation officer at NTNC Gobinda Prasad Pokharel said that repeated efforts, from parliamentary recommendations to government committees, have struggled to produce lasting results, often due to weak coordination and a lack of site-specific strategies. Relocation, often used as a management response, has repeatedly shifted the problem rather than resolving it.

A 2026 review of Nepal’s relief policies for wildlife-induced damage, published in Banko Janakari , found that compensation mechanisms have struggled to keep pace with the scale of conflict, with provisions unevenly applied and often inaccessible to the communities most affected.

On May 18, 2026, Nepal’s Ministry of Agriculture, Forests and Environment formed a 15-member technical task force to address human–wildlife conflict, joining the parliamentary recommendations and past committees that have previously tackled the issue. According to Shila Gyawali, under-secretary at the ministry and the member responsible for coordinating the task force, the group includes representatives from key government agencies, universities and conservation organizations, along with subject experts.

It has been tasked with reviewing existing policies, research and compensation systems, and recommending short-, medium- and long-term measures. The task force is collecting feedback from government bodies, experts and farmers, and is expected to submit its report within three months, including options to improve relief mechanisms and test pilot interventions in affected areas.

The IUCN, in its guidelines on human-wildlife conflict and coexistence, emphasizes that there is no single definitive solution, and that responses must be tailored to local ecological and social conditions — a conclusion that has repeatedly proven true in Nepal.

Back in Birta Deurali, Tamang and Lama face another season. Each morning begins with scanning the trees, listening for movement, hoping the crops survive another day. A few hundred meters away, the macaques are making the same calculations they always have, moving toward the easiest food source available.

शोधकर्ता एआई आधारित डिटेक्शन सिस्टम के परीक्षण करी रहलऽ छै, जेकरा म॑ स॑ एक म॑ लगभग ८८% फील्ड एक्यूरेसी हासिल करलऽ गेलऽ छै ।

नेपाल केरऽ मुआवजा आरू स्थानांतरण नीति संघर्ष के साथ चलै लेली संघर्ष करी रहलऽ छै, आरू मई २०२६ म॑ बनलऽ १५ सदस्यीय सरकारी टास्क फोर्स न॑ अभी तलक रिपोर्ट नै करलकै, जेकरा चलतें किसानऽ क॑ ई बीच भोर म॑ अपनऽ खेतऽ के पहरा देना छै । सब प्रमुख विचार देखें
